sourceMovie.title: I Want to Eat Your Pancreas
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: I Want to Eat Your Pancreas (2018) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Shinichiro Ushijima's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Your Name.
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Your Name. (2016) is the natural next step after I Want to Eat Your Pancreas: same animation DNA, different perspective. The bittersweet streak and drama elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: A Silent Voice: The Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of I Want to Eat Your Pancreas who respond to its animation will find A Silent Voice: The Movie (2016) immediately familiar. Both films share a drama sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dreaming Girl
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason I Want to Eat Your Pancreas fans keep landing on Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dreaming Girl (2019) — the animation is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: The Anthem of the Heart
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Anthem of the Heart draws from the same animation well as I Want to Eat Your Pancreas, channeling drama in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2015 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Ride Your Wave
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Ride Your Wave (2019) captures the animation appeal of I Want to Eat Your Pancreas without simply imitating it. The drama streak running through both films is what puts Ride Your Wave at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The animation appeal of I Want to Eat Your Pancreas — that bittersweet edge, the drama — translates cleanly into The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes (2022). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Breakfast at Tiffany's
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Breakfast at Tiffany's (1961) clearly understood what makes drama work — the same quality that keeps I Want to Eat Your Pancreas rewatchable. Expect the same funny rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: When Marnie Was There
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: When Marnie Was There (2014) is the kind of film I Want to Eat Your Pancreas fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The animation overlap is real, but it's the uplifting emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: The Big Blue
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Big Blue shares I Want to Eat Your Pancreas's gift for drama — the 1988 film delivers romance without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Talk to Her
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes I Want to Eat Your Pancreas compelling — the drama, the romance — shows up in Talk to Her (2002) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that bittersweet storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

faq.question: What should I watch after I Want to Eat Your Pancreas?
faq.answer: Try Your Name., A Silent Voice: The Movie, Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dreaming Girl—each shares DNA with I Want to Eat Your Pancreas while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is I Want to Eat Your Pancreas?
faq.answer: I Want to Eat Your Pancreas (2018) is best described as Animation, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is I Want to Eat Your Pancreas worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
